commands.review_summary:
The PR freezes provider quota card models during manual refresh, evaluates quota timing against a refresh-aware timestamp, clears frozen state on completion/shutdown, and adds Codex/Claude regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main returns the fallback menu-card model while manual refresh is in flight, so a fallback rebuilt from intermediate store data can appear under the Refreshing subtitle.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor/maintainer real behavior proof rather than an automated code repair.

target.title: Fix manual refresh quota display
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a manual refresh display issue where quota values, reset countdowns, or pace text could temporarily show stale or inconsistent data while the menu still showed `Refreshing…`.

The refresh UI now freezes the current visible quota card model when manual refresh starts, then keeps using that frozen model until refresh completes. This is provider-generic and covers both Claude and Codex paths.

## Why

During manual refresh, the menu immediately switched to `Refreshing…`, but parts of the quota card could still be rebuilt from the store while the refresh was in flight. If the store exposed stale or intermediate snapshot data, the UI could temporarily show a different quota value before the refresh completed.

## Details

- Freeze manual-refresh card models by `UsageProvider`.
- Keep frozen quota card models visible until manual refresh finishes.
- Clear frozen models when refresh ends or app shutdown cleanup runs.
- Use a refresh-aware quota timestamp so reset/pace text is evaluated against the snapshot timestamp while refreshing.
- Align Codex projection and weekly pace evaluation with that same timestamp.
- Replace duplicate-sensitive dictionary construction with a safer loop.
- Add regression coverage for Codex and Claude.

## Validation

- `swift test --filter StatusMenuPersistentRefreshTests`
- `swift test --filter MenuCardRefreshTests`
- `make check`
target.author: zihao-qi
